title: METABOLOMICS EFFECTS OF FOLDING CORRECTION IN RETINITIS PIGMENTOSA RHODOPSIN MUTANT P23A
authors_full: Meghana Hosahalli Shivananda Murthy¹, Hannah Staggs⁴, Paniz Jasbi², Philip J. Reeves⁴ and Judith Klein-Seetharaman¹,²,³
authors_short: Meghana Hosahalli Shivananda Murthy Hannah Staggs Paniz Jasbi Philip J. Reeves & Judith Klein-Seetharaman

summary.hook: This poster tests how rhodopsin misfolding (retinitis pigmentosa mutant P23A) changes cellular metabolism, and whether a “structural rescue” variant (P23A/N2C/D282C) restores...
summary.summary_30s: This poster tests how rhodopsin misfolding (retinitis pigmentosa mutant P23A) changes cellular metabolism, and whether a “structural rescue” variant (P23A/N2C/D282C) restores metabolism toward wild-type.

key_numbers: This poster tests how rhodopsin misfolding (retinitis pigmentosa mutant P23A) changes cellular metabolism, and whether a...
takeaways: RP background: RP affects \~1 in 3,000 individuals worldwide; currently no cure.
P23A misfolding reduces stable rhodopsin expression; structural correction (P23A/N2C/D282C) restores expression levels comparable to WT (Western blot, 3 experiments).
Protein misfolding (P23A rhodopsin) is associated with broad metabolic suppression in this cell model, and correcting folding partially normalizes metabolism.

Why did you choose tetracycline (2 µg/mL) and NaBu (5 mM) for 24 h specifically—were these optimized for expression vs metabolic stability?
